title: Ampelaster carolinianus
intro: Ampelaster carolinianus, also known as Carolina rose, is a flowering shrub native to the southeastern United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, making it a potential natural remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Ampelaster carolinianus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to aid in digestion, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Ampelaster carolinianus is a flowering plant species native to the southeastern United States. It belongs to the family Calycanthaceae, which consists of about 27 species of flowering plants. The genus Ampelaster is a relatively small group with only one described species, A. carolinianus. This plant is closely related to the genus Sinocalycanthus and shares similarities with the family Calycanthaceae. Ampelaster carolinianus is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens and landscapes due to its unique flowers and attractive foliage.
uses: Ampelaster carolinianus, also known as the Carolina rose, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
